question_text: Two permanent magnets are placed near each other with their north poles facing. What will happen and why?
correct_answer: The magnets will repel each other because like poles (both north) repel. The magnetic field lines from each magnet push against each other, creating a repulsive force.
explanation: Like magnetic poles always repel each other, while opposite poles attract. This is because the magnetic field lines around a north pole push outward and cannot merge with the outward-pushing field lines of another north pole. This property is used in many applications like maglev trains.
